prompt_preview:
You are an expert AI teaching assistant creating an educational presentation.

Generate exactly 12 s
response:
{
  "slides":[
      {
         "slide_type":"intro",
         "title":"Introduction to Decision Trees",
         "bullets":[
            {"text":"Decision trees are widely used models for classification and regression tasks","source_id":"Page 84"},
            {"text":"They learn a hierarchy of if/else questions, leading to a decision","source_id":"Page 84"},
            {"text":"These questions are similar to the questions you might ask in a game of 20 Questions","source_id":"Page 84"},
            {"text":"Decision trees are useful when training even a linear model might take too long","source_id":"Page 84"}
         ],
         "key_message":"Decision trees are a type of machine learning model that learn a hierarchy of if/else questions to make predictions",
         "visual_hint":"hierarchy",
         "image_id":null,
         "speaker_notes":"Start by introducing the concept of decision trees and their application in machine learning"
      },
      {
         "slide_type":"definition",
         "title":"Definition of Decision Trees",
         "bullets":[
            {"text":"A decision tree is a tree-like model where each internal node represents a feature or attribute","source_id":"Page 85"},
            {"text":"Each leaf node represents a class label or prediction","source_id":"Page 85"},
            {"text":"The tree is constructed by recursively partitioning the data into smaller subsets","source_id":"Page 85"},
            {"text":"The goal is to find the best sequence of if/else questions to get to the true answer most quickly","source_id":"Page 85"}
         ],
         "key_message":"A decision tree is a tree-like model that recursively partitions the data to make predictions",
         "visual_hint":"flowchart",
         "image_id":null,
         "speaker_notes":"Explain the definition of a decision tree and how it works"
      },
      {
         "slide_type":"concept",
         "title":"How Decision Trees Work",
         "bullets":[
            {"text":"The algorithm starts at the root node and selects the best feature to split the data","source_id":"Page 85"},
            {"text":"The data is then split into two subsets based on the selected feature","source_id":"Page 85"},
            {"text":"This process is repeated recursively until a stopping criterion is met","source_id":"Page 85"},
            {"text":"The stopping criterion can be a maximum depth or a minimum number of samples per node","source_id":"Page 85"}
         ],
         "key_message":"Decision trees work by recursively splitting the data into smaller subsets based on the best feature",
         "visual_hint":"process",
         "image_id":null,
         "speaker_notes":"Explain the process of how decision trees work"
      },
      {
         "slide_type":"example",
         "title":"Example of a Decision Tree",
         "bullets":[
            {"text":"Consider a dataset of animals with features such as number of legs and type of skin","source_id":"Page 84"},
            {"text":"The decision tree would start by asking if the animal has four legs","source_id":"Page 84"},
            {"text":"If the answer is yes, the tree would then ask if the animal has fur","source_id":"Page 84"},
            {"text":"If the answer is yes, the tree would predict that the animal is a bear","source_id":"Page 84"}
         ],
         "key_message":"Decision trees can be used to classify objects into different categories based on their features",
         "visual_hint":"hierarchy",
         "image_id":null,
         "speaker_notes":"Use an example to illustrate how decision trees can be used in practice"
      },
      {
         "slide_type":"example",
         "title":"Two Moons Dataset Example",
         "bullets":[
            {"text":"The two moons dataset consists of two half-moon shapes, with each class consisting of 75 data points","source_id":"Page 85"},
            {"text":"The goal is to learn a decision tree that can classify the data points into the correct class","source_id":"Page 85"},
            {"text":"The decision tree would start by selecting the best feature to split the data","source_id":"Page 85"},
            {"text":"The tree would then recursively split the data until a stopping criterion is met","source_id":"Page 85"}
         ],
         "key_message":"Decision trees can be used to classify complex datasets such as the two moons dataset",
         "visual_hint":"none",
         "image_id":"IMG_013",
         "speaker_notes":"Use the two moons dataset to illustrate how decision trees can be used to classify complex data"
      },
      {
         "slide_type":"concept",
         "title":"Bootstrap Sampling",
         "bullets":[
            {"text":"Bootstrap sampling is a technique used to create new datasets from the original dataset","source_id":"Page 98"},
            {"text":"A possible bootstrap sample would be a random selection of data points with replacement","source_id":"Page 98"},
            {"text":"The decision tree algorithm is then run on the bootstrap sample","source_id":"Page 98"},
            {"text":"This process is repeated multiple times to create multiple decision trees","source_id":"Page 98"}
         ],
         "key_message":"Bootstrap sampling is a technique used to create new datasets and improve the robustness of decision trees",
         "visual_hint":"process",
         "image_id":null,
         "speaker_notes":"Explain the concept of bootstrap sampling and its application in decision trees"
      },
      {
         "slide_type":"concept",
         "title":"Random Feature Selection",
         "bullets":[
            {"text":"Random feature selection is a technique used to select a subset of features to consider at each node","source_id":"Page 98"},
            {"text":"The number of features to select is controlled by the max_features parameter","source_id":"Page 98"},
            {"text":"The decision tree algorithm then selects the best feature to split the data from the selected subset","source_id":"Page 98"},
            {"text":"This process is repeated recursively until a stopping criterion is met","source_id":"Page 98"}
         ],
         "key_message":"Random feature selection is a technique used to improve the robustness of decision trees by reducing overfitting",
         "visual_hint":"flowchart",
         "image_id":null,
         "speaker_notes":"Explain the concept of random feature selection and its application in decision trees"
      },
      {
         "slide_type":"example",
         "title":"Decision Tree with Depth 1",
         "bullets":[
            {"text":"A decision tree with depth 1 has only one level of splitting","source_id":"Page 87"},
            {"text":"The tree splits the data into two subsets based on the best feature","source_id":"Page 87"},
            {"text":"The decision boundary of the tree is a simple line or hyperplane","source_id":"Page 87"},
            {"text":"The tree is simple and easy to interpret, but may not be accurate for complex datasets","source_id":"Page 87"}
         ],
         "key_message":"A decision tree with depth 1 is a simple tree that splits the data into two subsets based on the best feature",
         "visual_hint":"none",
         "image_id":"IMG_008",
         "speaker_notes":"Use an example to illustrate the concept of a decision tree with depth 1"
      },
      {
         "slide_type":"example",
         "title":"Decision Tree with Depth 2",
         "bullets":[
            {"text":"A decision tree with depth 2 has two levels of splitting","source_id":"Page 87"},
            {"text":"The tree splits the data into four subsets based on the best two features","source_id":"Page 87"},
            {"text":"The decision boundary of the tree is a more complex shape than a simple line or hyperplane","source_id":"Page 87"},
            {"text":"The tree is more accurate than a tree with depth 1, but may be more difficult to interpret","source_id":"Page 87"}
         ],
         "key_message":"A decision tree with depth 2 is a more complex tree that splits the data into four subsets based on the best two features",
         "visual_hint":"hierarchy",
         "image_id":"IMG_009",
         "speaker_notes":"Use an example to illustrate the concept of a decision tree with depth 2"
      },
      {
         "slide_type":"comparison",
         "title":"Comparison of Decision Trees and Other Models",
         "bullets":[
            {"text":"Decision trees are similar to other machine learning models such as logistic regression and support vector machines","source_id":"Page 85"},
            {"text":"However, decision trees are often more interpretable and easier to understand than these models","source_id":"Page 85"},
            {"text":"Decision trees are also often more robust to outliers and missing data than these models","source_id":"Page 85"},
            {"text":"However, decision trees can be more prone to overfitting than these models","source_id":"Page 85"}
         ],
         "key_message":"Decision trees have both advantages and disadvantages compared to other machine learning models",
         "visual_hint":"comparison",
         "image_id":null,
         "speaker_notes":"Compare decision trees to other machine learning models and highlight their advantages and disadvantages"
      },
      {
         "slide_type":"concept",
         "title":"Advantages of Decision Trees",
         "bullets":[
            {"text":"Decision trees are often easy to interpret and understand","source_id":"Page 85"},
            {"text":"Decision trees are often robust to outliers and missing data","source_id":"Page 85"},
            {"text":"Decision trees can handle both categorical and numerical data","source_id":"Page 85"},
            {"text":"Decision trees are often fast to train and can handle large datasets","source_id":"Page 85"}
         ],
         "key_message":"Decision trees have several advantages that make them a popular choice for many machine learning tasks",
         "visual_hint":"mindmap",
         "image_id":null,
         "speaker_notes":"Highlight the advantages of decision trees and explain why they are a popular choice for many machine learning tasks"
      },
      {
         "slide_type":"summary",
         "title":"Summary of Decision Trees",
         "bullets":[
            {"text":"Decision trees are a type of machine learning model that learn a hierarchy of if/else questions to make predictions","source_id":"Page 84"},
            {"text":"Decision trees are often easy to interpret and understand, and are robust to outliers and missing data","source_id":"Page 85"},
            {"text":"Decision trees can handle both categorical and numerical data, and are often fast to train","source_id":"Page 85"},
            {"text":"However, decision trees can be prone to overfitting, and may not be as accurate as other models for complex datasets","source_id":"Page 85"}
         ],
         "key_message":"Decision trees are a powerful and popular machine learning model that can be used for a wide range of tasks",
         "visual_hint":"none",
         "image_id":"IMG_001",
         "speaker_notes":"Summarize the key points of decision trees and highlight their importance in machine learning"
      }
   ]
}
